title: Common Alternative Names for a Project Initiation Document (PID)
summary: A Project Initiation Document (PID) is a formal record of a project's purpose, scope, and governance. This topic lists the most frequently used alternative terms—such as Project Charter and Project Brief—across various project‑management methodologies.
content: A Project Initiation Document (PID) is a formal record that captures essential project information at the outset, including purpose, objectives, scope, and governance. The PID is referred to by several alternative names depending on the methodology, the most common being Project Charter and Project Brief. While the terminology varies, the underlying intent of the document remains the same.
can_answer: What alternative names are used for a Project Initiation Document?
Is a Project Charter the same as a PID?
